query: BTS Gwanghwamun Square concert March 2026 Netflix livestream viewer count

mako.raw:
{"query":"BTS Gwanghwamun Square concert March 2026 Netflix livestream viewer count","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1808,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.billboard.com/music/pop/bts-comeback-live-18-4-million-views-netflix-1236206297/","title":"BTS’s ‘Comeback Live’ Drew 18.4 Million Views on Netflix","content":"BTS’s livestreamed comeback concert captured 18.4 million global viewers on Netflix, the streaming giant reports.
The one-hour show was beamed out from the superstar boyband’s performance Saturday (March 21) in their native Seoul, South Korea, an event that marked the first time all seven members had performed together since October 2022.
Some 100,000 members of BTS’ ARMY were watching on at the historic Gwanghwamun Square, as RM, Jin, SUGA, j-hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook showcased their latest album ARIRANG, which dropped on the previous day, on Friday.
The Netflix special nabbed top spot in 24 countries, according to a statement issued Wednesday (March 25), and cracked the weekly top 10 in 80 countries.
On the same day as the show, Spotify announced that ARIRANG, the boy band’s first album in six years, had become the platform’s most-streamed album in a single day in 2026 so far.
After answering fan questions and performing at a Spotify event in New York City, the singers will appear on NBC’s The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon on consecutive nights.
The lads will sit down with Fallon for a group chat and a performance on Wednesday (March 25), and return the following evening for another performance from ARIRANG.
The long-awaited return of BTS could provide HYBE with a massive financial boost in 2026, with Billboard estimating that the new album and tour could generate more than $1 billion in revenue from concerts, merchandise and licensing, album sales and streaming over a 12-month period."},{"url":"https://www.chosun.com/english/kpop-culture-en/2026/03/25/ZJ7WGKT3CBEUDEOUFQ53UYVZUE/","title":"BTS Comeback Concert Drawn 18.4 Million Global Viewers","content":"It was confirmed that 18.4 million people globally watched the live broadcast of BTS’s comeback concert at Seoul’s Gwanghwamun Square via Netflix.
According to Netflix on the 25th, the live-streamed event ‘BTS Comeback Live: ARIRANG’ on the 21st was viewed by 18.4 million people worldwide on the same day.
The viewing count (viewing hours divided by runtime) for ‘BTS Comeback Live: ARIRANG’ last week was 13.1 million, making it the most-watched non-English TV content from March 16–22.
According to Netflix, BTS-related content on its official channel has been exposed over 2.62 billion times.
This concert special was their first official reunion stage,” highlighting why global viewers paid attention.
BTS fully reunited on the 20th after 3 years and 9 months with their fifth album ‘ARIRANG’ and held the comeback commemorative performance ‘BTS Comeback Live: ARIRANG’ at 8 p.m. on the 21st at Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul."}]}
